summary:
I've found and fixed the needle in the haystack. The issue was a timing attack vulnerability in the password hash comparison function in `app/auth/compare.go`. The original code used a direct string comparison (`==`), which is not constant-time and could leak timing information to attackers.

I replaced it with `crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare`, which performs the comparison in constant time regardless of the input, preventing timing side-channel attacks. After making this change, I ran `./test.sh` again, and all security tests passed, confirming that the authentication is now constant-time and secure.
